data: Revise how to talk about past events in conversation

message: Let's revise how to talk about past events naturally in English.
image_url:
button_text: Continue!

message: Remember the structure you learned:

"Last [time period], I [action] and it was a great experience."

This structure uses the simple past tense to help you share memories in an engaging way.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/09cd2eab-d8ca-488f-97ab-51485f073820-54.-Talking-about-Past-Events.jpg
button_text: Next!

message: Great! Now let's practice using this structure again.
image_url: https://r2-activity-data.gosupernova.live/system-uploads/scenario-data/09cd2eab-d8ca-488f-97ab-51485f073820-54.-Talking-about-Past-Events.jpg
button_text: Start Practice!

Select the correct way to share a past experience with a friend

1.Last Friday, I attended a live music concert and it was a great experience.
2.I saw a concert not long ago and it was okay but nothing special.

Correct answer: 1

Select the correct way to describe something you did recently

1.Last summer, I completed an online course and it was a great experience.
2.I did an online course sometime ago and it was not too bad.

Correct answer: 1
Last&&Friday, I&&attended a&&live&&music&&concert and&&it&&was a&&great experience.
Last&&summer, I&&completed an&&online&&course and&&it&&was a&&great experience.
Last&&evening, I&&joined a&&group&&fitness&&session and&&it&&was a&&great experience.
Last&&Tuesday, I&&visited a&&book&&fair&&in&&the&&city and&&it&&was a&&great experience.
Last&&year, I&&went&&on a&&solo&&trip&&to&&the&&mountains and&&it&&was a&&great experience.

first_message: That was incredible! Now we're going to practice by answering few questions in English. Can we start?
prompt_id: 4d3f5213-fcc4-40f3-879d-051908377a31
questions: "'1. A colleague asks how your festival holiday was. How do you describe it?'

'2. Your friend asks what you did last weekend. How do you share the experience?'

'3. A manager asks about a recent training session you attended. How do you talk about it?'

'4. Your family asks what you did at a recent office event. How do you describe it?'

'5. A friend asks if anything interesting happened last month. How do you talk about it?'"
